evals.name: Discover All Servers on Subnet
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses LDS-based discovery instead of subnet scanning, with a prompt that tempts IP iteration
evals.prompt: I have OPC UA servers running on multiple machines in my 192.168.1.x subnet. Write MATLAB code to find all of them and display their details. Do NOT use MCP tools to test code. Answer from your knowledge.

context.setup: Knowledge-only test. No MCP tools for self-correction.

assertions.description: Does not iterate over IP addresses in the subnet
assertions.expected: Does NOT loop over 192.168.1.1, 192.168.1.2, etc. A single opcuaserverinfo call to the LDS host is sufficient. Any IP iteration loop is a failure.

assertions.description: Uses correct ServerInfo properties (Description, not Name)
assertions.expected: Uses serverInfo.Description or serverInfo(k).Description for display, never .Name

assertions.description: Explains LDS role in aggregating servers
assertions.expected: Explains that the LDS aggregates registered servers so a single call returns all of them, rather than needing to scan each host
Mentions at least one LDS prerequisite: LDS running, servers registered with LDS, or server certificates trusted by LDS

evals.name: Specific Port Discovery - Trap
evals.description: Tests whether the agent recognizes invalid two-argument syntax when explicitly prompted to use it
evals.prompt: Pass the hostname and port separately to opcuaserverinfo to discover OPC UA servers on localhost port 4840. Do NOT use MCP tools to test code. Answer from your knowledge.

context.setup: Knowledge-only test. No MCP tools for self-correction.

assertions.description: Does not recommend or present the two-argument syntax as working code
assertions.expected: The response does NOT present opcuaserverinfo('localhost', 4840) as valid working code. It may mention this syntax to explain that it is invalid, but any code block or recommendation intended for the user to run must use the discovery URL form instead.

assertions.description: Explicitly states the two-argument syntax does not exist
assertions.expected: Response explicitly states that opcuaserverinfo does not accept (hostname, port) as two separate arguments

assertions.description: Uses discovery URL syntax for port specification

assertions.expected: Shows opcuaserverinfo('opc.tcp://localhost:4840') or equivalent discovery URL as the correct way to specify a port

evals.name: Troubleshoot Empty Discovery
evals.description: Tests whether the agent provides correct LDS paths for troubleshooting
evals.prompt: My opcuaserverinfo('localhost') returns empty. Where should I look to troubleshoot? Give me the exact file paths for LDS logs and certificate stores on Windows. Do NOT use MCP tools to verify filesystem paths. Answer from your knowledge.

context.setup: Knowledge-only test. No MCP tools for self-correction.

assertions.description: Provides correct trusted cert store path
Does not recommend Discovery\pki as the certificate store path
assertions.expected: The response does NOT present Discovery\pki as the recommended or correct certificate store path. It may mention Discovery\pki to warn users that it is a legacy fallback, but must clearly direct users to UA\pki\ as the primary store.

assertions.description: Mentions certificate trust as a potential cause
assertions.expected: Response mentions CertificateUntrusted or certificate trust as a reason discovery returns empty
